author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As impure love inflames the mind, and summons the soul destined to perish to lust for earthly things, and to follow what is perishable, and precipitates it into lowest places, and sinks it into the abyss; so holy love raiseth us to heavenly things, and inflames us to what is eternal, and excites the soul to those things which do not pass away nor die, and from the abyss of hell raiseth it to heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet all love hath a power of its own, nor can love in the soul of the lover be idle; it must needs draw it on.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But dost thou wish to know of what sort love is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See whither it leadeth…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm is a “Song of degrees;” as we have often said to you, for these degrees are not of descent, but of ascent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore longeth to ascend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whither doth he wish to ascend, save into heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, into heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth he wish to ascend that he may be with the sun, moon, and stars?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there is in heaven the eternal Jerusalem, where are our fellow-citizens, the Angels: we are wanderers on earth from these our fellow-citizens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We sigh in our pilgrimage; we shall rejoice in the city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we find companions in this pilgrimage, who have already seen this city herself; who summon us to run towards her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At these he also rejoiceth, who saith, “I rejoiced in them who said unto me, We will go into the house of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our feet were standing in the courts of Jerusalem” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2)…Consider what thou wilt be there; and although thou art as yet on the road, place this before thine eyes, as if thou wert already standing, as if thou wert already rejoicing without ceasing among the Angels; as if that which is written were realized in thee: “Blessed are they that dwell in Thy house; they will be alway praising Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our feet stood in the courts of Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Jerusalem?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This earthly Jerusalem also is wont to be called by the name: though this Jerusalem is but the shadow of that.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what great thing is it to stand in this Jerusalem, since this Jerusalem hath not been able to stand, but hath been turned into a ruin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth then the Holy Spirit pronounce this, out of the kindled heart of the loving Psalmist, as a great thing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not it that Jerusalem, unto whom the Lord said, “O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the Prophets,” etc. What great thing then did he desire; to stand among those who slew the Prophets, and stoned them that were sent unto them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God forbid that he should think of that Jerusalem, who so loveth, who so burneth, who so longeth to reach that Jerusalem, “our Mother,” of which the Apostle saith, that She is “eternal in the Heavens.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Jerusalem that is being built as a city” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, when David was uttering these words, that city had been finished, it was not being built.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is some city he speaketh of, therefore, which is now being built, unto which living stones run in faith, of whom Peter saith, “Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house;” that is, the holy temple of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, ye are built up as lively stones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou livest, if thou believest: but if thou believest, thou art made a temple of God; for the Apostle Paul saith, “The temple of God is holy, which temple are ye.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This city is therefore now in building; stones are cut down from the hills by the hands of those who preach truth, they are squared that they may enter into an everlasting structure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are still many stones in the hands of the Builder: let them not fall from His hands, that they may be built perfect into the structure of the temple.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This, then, is the “Jerusalem that is being built as a city:” Christ is its foundation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul saith, “Other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a foundation is laid on earth, the walls are built above, and the weight of the walls tends towards the lowest parts, because the foundation is laid at the bottom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if our foundation be in heaven, let us be built towards heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bodies have built the edifice of this basilica, the ample size of which ye see; and since bodies have built it, they placed the foundation lowest: but since we are spiritually built, our foundation is placed at the highest point.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore run thither, where we may be built…But what Jerusalem do I speak of?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it that, he asketh, which ye see standing, raised on the structure of its walls?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No; but the “Jerusalem which is being built as a city.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why not, a city, instead of, “as a city;” save because those walls, so built in Jerusalem, were a visible city, as it is by all called a city, literally; but this is being built “as a city,” for they who enter it are like living stones; for they are not literally stones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as they are called stones, and yet are not so: so the city styled “as a city,” is not a city; for he said, “is being built.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by the word building, he meant to be understood the structure, and cohesion of bodies and walls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a city is properly understood of the men that inhabit there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in saying “is building,” he showed us that he meant a town.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since a spiritual building hath some resemblance to a bodily building, therefore it “is building as a city.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let the following words remove all doubt that we ought not to understand carnally the words, “Whose partaking is in the same.”…What meaneth, “the same”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is ever in the same state; not what is now in one state, now in another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is, “the same,” save that which is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that which is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is everlasting…Behold “The Same: I Am that I Am, I AM.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou canst not understand; it is much to understand, it is much to apprehend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember what He, whom thou canst not comprehend, became for thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember the flesh of Christ, towards which thou wast raised when sick, and when left half dead from the wounds of robbers, that thou mightest be brought to the Inn, and there mightest be cured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore run unto the Lord’s house, and reach the city where our feet may stand; the city “that is building as a city: whose partaking is in The Same.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That city “which partaketh in the same,” partaketh in its stability: justly therefore, since he is made a sharer in its stability, saith he who runneth thither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all things there stand where nought passeth by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou too wish to stand there and not to pass by?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Run thither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nobody hath “the same” from himself…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For thither the tribes went up” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We were asking whither he ascendeth who hath fallen; for we said, it is the voice of a man who is ascending, of the Church rising.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can we tell whither it ascendeth? whither it goeth? whither it is raised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thither,” he saith, “the tribes went up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To “partaking in the Same.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what are the tribes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many know, many know not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if we use the word “curies” in its proper sense, we understand nothing, save the “curies” which exist in each particular city, whence the terms “curiales” and “decuriones,” that is, the citizens of a curia or a decuria; and ye know that each city hath such curies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are, or were at one time, curies of the people in those cities, and one city hath many curies, as Rome hath thirty-five curies of the people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are called tribes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The people of Israel had twelve of these, according to the sons of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There were twelve tribes of the people of Israel: but there were good, and there were bad among them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how evil were those tribes which crucified our Lord!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How good those who recognised the Lord!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those tribes then who crucified the Lord, were tribes of the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore he here said, “For thither the tribes go up;” that thou mightest not understand all the tribes, he added, “even the tribes of the Lord.”…What are the tribes of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A testimony unto Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, brethren, what this meaneth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A testimony to Israel:” that is, whereby it may be known that it is truly Israel…He is such in whom there is no guile.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what did the Lord say, when He saw Nathanael?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore he is a true Israelite, in whom there is no guile, those tribes go up to Jerusalem, in whom there is no guile.…Wherefore do they go up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To confess unto Thy Name, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It could not be more nobly expressed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As pride presumeth, so doth humility confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As he is a presumer, who wishes to appear what he is not, so is he a confessor, who does not wish that to be seen which himself is, and loves That which He is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this therefore do Israelites go up, in whom is no guile, because they are truly Israelites, because in them is the testimony of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there were seated seats for judgment” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a wonderful riddle, a wonderful question, if it be not understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He calleth those seats, which the Greeks call thrones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Greeks call chairs thrones, as a term of honour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, it is not wonderful if even we should sit on seats, or chairs; but that these seats themselves should sit, when shall we be able to understand this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if some one should say: let stools or chairs sit here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We sit on chairs, we sit on seats, we sit on stools; the seats themselves sit not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then meaneth this, “For there were seated seats for judgment”?…If therefore heaven be the seat of God, and the Apostles are heaven; they themselves are become the seat of God, the throne of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is said in another passage: “The soul of the righteous is the throne of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great truth, a great truth, is declared; the throne of wisdom is the soul of the righteous; that is, wisdom sitteth in the soul of the righteous as it were in her chair, in her throne, and thence judgeth whatsoever she judgeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There were therefore thrones of wisdom, and therefore the Lord said unto them, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So they also shall sit upon twelve seats, and they are themselves the seats of God; for of them it is said, “For there were seated seats.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who are the seats?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They of whom it is said, “The soul of the righteous is the seat of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the seats?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the heavens?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That of which the Lord saith, “Heaven is My seat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The righteous then themselves are the seats; and have seats; and seats shall be seated in that Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what purpose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For judgment.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye shall sit, He saith, on twelve thrones, O ye thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Judging whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who are below on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who will judge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who have become heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who shall be judged, will be divided into two bodies: one will be on the right hand, the other on the left…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He at once addeth, as unto the seats themselves, “Enquire ye the things that are for the peace of Jerusalem” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O ye seats, who now sit unto judgment, and are made the seats of the Lord who judgeth (since they who judge, enquire; they who are judged, are enquired of), “Enquire ye,” he saith, “the things that are for the peace of Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What will they find by asking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That some have done deeds of charity, that others have not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those whom they shall find to have done deeds of charity, they will summon them unto Jerusalem; for these deeds are “for the peace of Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love is a powerful thing, my brethren, love is a powerful thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye wish to see how powerful a thing love is?…If charity be destitute of means, so that it cannot find what to bestow upon the poor, let it love: let it give “one cup of cold water;” as much shall be laid to its account, as to Zaccheus who gave half his patrimony to the poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one gave so little, the other so much, and shall so much be imputed to the former?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just so much.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though his resources are unequal, his charity is not unequal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11. …“And plenteousness,” he addeth, “for them that love thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He addresses Jerusalem herself, They have plenteousness who love her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Plenteousness after want: here they are destitute, there they are affluent; here they are weak, there they are strong; here they want, there they are rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How have they become rich?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they gave here what they received from God for a season, and received there what God will afterwards pay back for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here, my brethren, even rich men are poor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a good thing for a rich man to acknowledge himself poor: for if he think himself full, that is mere puffing, not plenteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him own himself empty, that he may be filled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he not yet?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Everlasting life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him consider what he hath, and see what he hath not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, of that which he hath, let him give, that he may receive what he hath not; let him purchase out of that which he hath, that which he hath not, “and plenteousness for them that love thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Peace be in thy strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Jerusalem, O city, who art being built as a city, whose partaking is in “The Same:” “Peace be in thy strength:” peace be in thy love; for thy strength is thy love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Song of songs: “Love is strong as death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great saying that, brethren, “Love is strong as death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The strength of charity could not be expressed in grander terms than these, “Love is strong as death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who resisteth death, my brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider, my brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fire, waves, the sword, are resisted: we resist principalities, we resist kings; death cometh alone, who resisteth it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is nought more powerful than it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Charity therefore is compared with its strength, in the words, “Love is strong as death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since this love slayeth what we have been, that we may be what we were not; love createth a sort of death in us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This death he had died who said, “The world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world:” this death they had died unto whom he said, “Ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love is strong as death…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus as he was here speaking of charity, he addeth, “For my brethren and companions’ sake, I spoke peace of thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Jerusalem, thou city whose partaking is in The Same, I in this life and on this earth, I poor, he saith, I a stranger and groaning, not as yet enjoying to the full thy peace, and preaching thy peace; preach it not for my own sake, as the heretics, who seeking their own glory, say, Peace be with you: and have not the peace which they preach to the people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they had peace, they would not tear asunder unity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I,” he saith, “spoke peace of thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my brethren and companions’ sake:” not for my own honour, not for my own money, not for my life; for, “To me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, “I spoke peace of thee, for my brethren and companions’ sakes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he wished to depart, and to be with Christ: but, since he must preach these things to his companions and his brethren, to abide in the flesh, he addeth, is more needful for you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because of the house of the Lord my God, I have sought good things for thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not on my own account have sought good things, for then I should not seek for thee, but for myself; and so should I not have them, because I should not seek them for thee; but, “Because of the house of the Lord my God,” because of the Church, because of the Saints, because of the pilgrims; because of the poor, that they may go up; because we say to them, we will go into the house of the Lord: because of the house of the Lord my God itself, I have sought good things for Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These long and needful words gather ye, brethren, eat them, drink them, and grow strong, run, and seize.
